data.title: Distributed Systems
data.description: Amazon's architecture relies heavily on distributed systems to manage its vast network of services and databases.
data.technical_spec: Microservices, data redundancy, fault tolerance, and load balancing.

data.title: Scalable Infrastructure
data.description: The platform is designed to scale rapidly in response to increased demand.
data.technical_spec: Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2), Auto Scaling, and DynamoDB.

data.title: Data Centers
data.description: Amazon utilizes numerous data centers globally to ensure low latency and high availability.
data.technical_spec: Geo-redundancy, data replication, and edge nodes.

data.title: Serverless Computing
data.description: Services like AWS Lambda are employed to provide serverless processing capabilities.

data.title: Integrated Content Delivery Network
data.description: Amazon CloudFront is used to deliver content with low latency and high transfer speeds.
